question_text: Which of the following statements correctly distinguishes between contact and non-contact forces?

options.label: A
options.text: Contact forces are always stronger than non-contact forces

options.label: B
options.text: Contact forces require objects to touch, whilst non-contact forces act over a distance

options.label: C
options.text: Non-contact forces only occur in space, whilst contact forces occur on Earth

options.label: D
options.text: Contact forces cause motion, whilst non-contact forces cause stationary objects to remain still

correct_answer: Contact forces require objects to touch, whilst non-contact forces act over a distance
explanation: Contact forces like friction and normal reaction only work when two surfaces are touching each other. Non-contact forces like gravity and magnetism can act across empty space without objects needing to touch. This is the key difference between the two types of forces.
